summary: Russian drones struck the Zaporozhye regional administration building and a gas station near Poltava, while Ukrainian forces damaged three bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk. Guided aviation bombs were repeatedly launched on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions. Ukrainian commander of the 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade was found dead.
key_points: A Russian drone attacked the Zaporozhye regional administration building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252).
Russian drones struck a gas station near Poltava (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31238).
Ukrainian forces damaged an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Guided aviation bombs were repeatedly launched on Zaporizhzhia city (Source: @kpszsu/66830).
Guided aviation bombs were launched on Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
The commander of Ukraine’s 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade was found dead (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).
Russian forces struck targets in Kharkov, producing a visible smoke column (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31250).
Strike UAVs were reported moving south‑east of Poltava heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66825).

sections.executive_summary: A Russian drone hit the Zaporozhye regional administration building, marking a direct strike on civil infrastructure. Ukraine responded by destroying three key bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics. Repeated guided aviation bomb launches targeted Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, indicating intensified aerial bombardment.

points: Ukrainian forces destroyed an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk oblast and two railway bridges in Luhansk oblast, which the enemy used for moving personnel, weapons and supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
The commander of the 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade was found dead; the circumstances are under investigation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).
Ukrainian troops struck targets in Kharkov, generating a column of smoke after an explosion (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31250).
Strike UAVs were observed moving south‑east of Poltava with a southern heading, indicating possible targeting of rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
UAV activity was reported towards Kotelva in Poltava region from the east, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/66793).
A Russian drone attacked the Zaporozhye regional administration building, striking a government facility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252).
Russian drones hit a gas station near Poltava, causing damage to civilian infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31238).
Guided aviation bombs were repeatedly launched on Zaporizhzhia city, indicating sustained aerial bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/66830).
Guided aviation bombs were also launched on Kharkiv region, expanding the strike envelope (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
UAVs were reported heading from the Black Sea northward toward Mykolaiv oblast, suggesting a maritime‑to‑land strike vector (Source: @kpszsu/66821).
Ukrainian sabotage of three bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk disrupts Russian supply lines across the front (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Repeated guided aviation bomb launches on Zaporizhzhia city target eastern industrial zones and transport hubs (Source: @kpszsu/66830).
UAVs observed moving eastward toward Kotelva in Poltava region, potentially preparing strikes on eastern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/66793).
Strike UAVs reported south‑east of Poltava heading south could threaten eastern rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
UAVs were sighted heading toward Meni in Chernihiv oblast from the north‑west, indicating activity near the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66812).
UAVs reported along the border in Chernihiv region near Dobryanka, suggesting reconnaissance along the northern boundary (Source: @eRadarrua/98707).
UAVs observed moving from the north toward Chutove in Poltava region, potentially probing northern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/66794).
UAVs reported heading toward Limanka in Odesa from the Black Sea, but this lies on the southwestern edge of the northern sector (Source: @kpszsu/66813).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea northward toward Mykolaiv oblast indicate a southern front pressure vector (Source: @kpszsu/66821).
Guided aviation bomb launches on Kharkiv region may affect southern approaches to the city (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
UAV activity reported in Zaporizhzhia region from the south, including Kab deployments (Source: @kpszsu/66809).
Strike UAVs moving south‑east of Poltava could threaten southern logistical corridors (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
Repeated guided aviation bomb releases on Zaporizhzhia city show intensified Russian air strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/66830).
Guided aviation bomb sorties over Kharkiv region demonstrate expanded Russian aerial targeting (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
UAV sightings across multiple oblasts (Poltava, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia) reflect a broad Russian UAV deployment strategy (Sources: @kpszsu/66825, @kpszsu/66812, @kpszsu/66830).
UAVs observed moving from the Black Sea toward Mykolaiv suggest maritime‑origin air operations (Source: @kpszsu/66821).

key_developments: Russian drone strike on Zaporozhye regional administration building, targeting civil governance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252).
Ukrainian demolition of an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Repeated guided aviation bomb launches on Zaporizhzhia city, indicating sustained aerial bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/66830).
Guided aviation bomb sorties over Kharkiv region, expanding Russian air strike envelope (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
UAVs moving south‑east of Poltava with a southern heading, suggesting targeting of rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
UAVs observed from the Black Sea northward toward Mykolaiv oblast, indicating maritime‑to‑land strike vector (Source: @kpszsu/66821).
Commander of Ukraine’s 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade found dead, potential impact on unit command (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and guided bomb deployments are intensifying across the eastern and southern fronts, targeting both civilian infrastructure and military positions. The strike on Zaporozhye's administration building demonstrates a willingness to hit governmental sites, while bridge destructions by Ukraine aim to degrade Russian supply lines. Continued aerial pressure on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv suggests a focus on disrupting logistics and command structures.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are leveraging bridge sabotage to impede Russian movements in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, while increasing UAV reconnaissance and strike activity in northern and eastern sectors. Russian air assets remain active, employing both drones and guided bombs to maintain pressure on key urban centers. Coordination between ground and air units appears heightened on both sides.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals a pattern of south‑eastward movements from Poltava and northward vectors from the Black Sea, indicating potential targeting of supply corridors. The death of the 154th Brigade commander may affect Ukrainian mechanized operations in the south. Repeated guided bomb usage suggests stockpiled munitions are being expended, which could influence future strike frequency.
